title: helianthemum sanguineum var. retrofractum
intro: Helianthemum sanguineum var. retrofractum, also known as rockrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Cistaceae family, commonly found in dry, rocky areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum sanguineum var. retrofractum include its potential to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using its extracts in ointments and salves for topical application.
